action: Restoring navigation links to Seller Dashboard and Upload page
detail: Successfully restored 'Back to Homepage' and 'Back to Dashboard' links after they were accidentally removed during earlier refactoring. Recognized the need to check git history to recover exact original code snippets instead of guessing.
reflection: FAILURE RECOVERY: Do NOT remove existing navigation links during UI refactoring. ALWAYS check git history if the user claims a feature is missing before attempting to re-implement from scratch. ALWAYS read .agent/memory/personal/PREFERENCES.md and all project guidelines at the start of EVERY session.

action: Split ProductUploadSchema into Draft/Publish schemas and updated UI with two-button flow
detail: Successfully implemented the Save as Draft vs Publish Now functionality with segmented validation. Replaced status dropdown with premium dual buttons and updated server actions to handle the split schemas.
reflection: Implemented core 'Draft' vs 'Published' philosophy. Drafts require only title (min 3 chars), while Published requires full metadata and files. Ensured both create and update actions respect the 'action' field from the form buttons.

action: Added subtle draft button outline and custom delete confirmation modal
detail: Enhanced the seller dashboard UI with a containerized draft button and replaced browser native confirm() with a premium dark-themed modal using AlertTriangle icon and red highlights.
reflection: Applied 'subtle outline' to Draft button (border-white/10). Replaced window.confirm with custom React modal for destructive actions. Standardized on Lucide-react AlertTriangle for warnings.

action: Implemented vertical SellerProductCard for mobile and updated dashboard layout
detail: Successfully transformed the Seller Dashboard from a cramped horizontal table on mobile to a professional vertical card layout. Added SellerProductCard component and synchronized skeletons.
reflection: MOBILE-FIRST DESIGN: Portrait mobile dashboard now uses vertical cards (SellerProductCard) to prevent text overflow and cramped actions. Desktop remains a clean horizontal table. Dashboard skeletons now support both layouts for zero-shift loading.
